name: Nantua Sauce - Great Chefs
recipeIngredient: Mirepoix is a mixture of diced onions, carrots, celery, and leeks.
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat the olive oil in a sauté pan over medium heat until very hot.
2. Add the live crayfish to the pan and saute for 1 minute.
3. Add the garlic bulb (halved), mirepoix, peppercorns, bay leaf, and button mushrooms. Cook for another minute.
4. Add the cognac and flame the pan by pouring in a small amount of cognac.
5. Pour the contents into a saucepan and mash with a spoon to combine.
6. To the saucepan with the crayfish, add the chopped tomato, parsley, tarragon, and tomato puree.
7. Deglaze the sauté pan with 4 cups of fish stock (enough to cover ingredients in the saucepan) and pour the contents of the deglazed pan into the saucepan with the crayfish and vegetables.
8. Roughly mash the contents again and cook for 20 minutes over high heat until boiling.
9. Strain the contents through a chinois into another sauté pan.
10. Reduce the liquid by two-thirds, then add the cream, salt, pepper, and cayenne pepper. Cook for an additional 5-10 minutes until reduced to half its original volume.
11. Strain again and reserve.
